dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to represent established range boundaries over multiple periods. Entry conditions trigger when price crosses the upper boundary (short) or lower boundary (long). Exit conditions are reversal crossings. Range duration ('a few days') approximated with 5-day max hold. The signal is context-dependent (LONG at lower boundary, SHORT at upper boundary), so both entry conditions are included.

backtest_rule.reason: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to represent established range boundaries over multiple periods. Entry conditions trigger when price crosses the upper boundary (short) or lower boundary (long). Exit conditions are reversal crossings. Range duration ('a few days') approximated with 5-day max hold. The signal is context-dependent (LONG at lower boundary, SHORT at upper boundary), so both entry conditions are included.

source_rule_text: When market moves sideways with clear range boundaries, go short at the top boundary and go long at the lower boundary of the range.
